title: Hydropark Widget — table (v1)
description: The `table` widget (SPEC 9.4 'table — structured data'): typed COLUMNS + ROWS, optional column SORT, and CELLS TYPED PER COLUMN. It `allOf`-extends the base widget contract (_widget-base.schema.json): the base envelope (id/title/region/priority/binds_state/reads_state/binds_tool/emits/states/a11y/style/min_widget_version) governs, and this schema pins `type` to the const `table`, closes `props` (columns + sort + selection config), and constrains `emits` to the table event set. It validates the CANONICAL widget object; a manifest may author the SHORTHAND projection (props inline; region/priority/emits/min_widget_version omitted) which the app normalizes before validating (base contract §1, SPEC 9.2). Binding fit: `binds_state` accepts a `list<record<…>>` slot — each column.key maps to a field of the row record (base contract §9; see $defs/bindsStateAcceptsKind). Read-first by default; a column may opt into inline edit, which is gated by writer-of-record (base contract §5).

type.description: Pins this instance to the `table` widget type (base $defs/knownWidgetTypeV1). The base keeps `type` a shape-checked string (not a closed enum) so a too-new type placeholders instead of failing; this const is the table-specific tightening applied via allOf.

props.description: Typed, immutable-per-instance configuration for the table (base contract §2). The row DATA is NOT authored here — it is the live value of the `binds_state` slot (a `list<record<…>>`), produced by tools/slots/user input and passed to the renderer as runtime state (see $defs/runtimeState).

columns.description: Ordered column definitions (left→right in LTR; mirrored under RTL by the layout engine). Each column names a record field via `key` and declares its cell TYPE. Column `key`s MUST be unique within the table — uniqueness across objects cannot be expressed structurally, so it is a certification check (P1-04.5), not enforced here by uniqueItems (which only rejects fully-identical column objects).

row_key.description: The row-record field used as the STABLE identity for diffing, sort-stability, selection, and inline-edit targeting. Defaults to `id` (the app-assigned stable entry id on `item`/record rows, base $defs/itemRecord). Must name a field present on every row record — a cross-artifact certification check.

sortable.description: Master switch for the OPTIONAL sort feature. `false` renders a static, non-sortable table (headers are plain `columnheader`s with no `aria-sort`, not focusable sort controls). Individual columns may still opt out via `column.sortable:false`.

default_sort.description: Optional initial sort applied at first render. Its `column` must reference an existing, sortable column `key` (certification check). Omitted → rows render in the slot's natural (insertion) order.

multi_sort.description: When true, the user may layer sort keys (primary, secondary, …) and each sorted header exposes its sort rank to assistive tech. When false, activating a new header replaces the sort. Ignored when `sortable:false`.

selectable.description: Row-selection mode. `none` (default) = display/sort only. `single`/`multiple` add a roving-focus selection affordance (checkbox/row-toggle) and enable the `selection_changed` event. Selection is RUNTIME state (session-scoped projection), not authored here.

row_activation.description: When true, activating a row (click, or Enter/Space on the focused row) invokes `binds_tool` deterministically with the row's `row_key` value — the UI-first path (base contract §3 dir 2) — and emits `row_activated`. Requires `binds_tool` to be set (certification check). When false, rows are not actionable.

paginate.description: When true, rows are paged in fixed windows of `page_size` with keyboard-reachable pager controls and `aria-rowcount` reflecting the FULL row count. When false, the full set renders in a scroll region (the renderer virtualizes large lists for performance); no data is truncated.

page_size.description: Rows per page when `paginate:true`. Ignored otherwise.

sticky_header.description: Keep the header row pinned while the body scrolls (layout behavior, not styling). Does not affect focus order or ARIA.

caption.description: Localizable table caption/summary. Rendered as the table's accessible caption (native `<caption>` semantics) describing what the rows represent — distinct from `a11y.description`. Also shown visually below the title when present.

binds_state.description: The primary shared-state slot this table renders. MUST resolve to a `list<record<…>>` slot (see $defs/bindsStateAcceptsKind): each element is one ROW; each `column.key` reads a field of the row record. Renders live on every slot update (base contract §3 dir 3). Editing is possible only when (a) a column sets `editable:true` AND (b) the owning skill is the slot's writer-of-record; otherwise the table binds READ-ONLY (renders live, edit affordances disabled, writer named — base contract §5).

reads_state.description: Additional slots observed READ-ONLY (base contract §4). A table may read a scalar/record slot to derive computed or annotation columns for the rows it does not own; an update to any listed slot re-renders the table. Never a write path.

binds_tool.description: The tool a user action invokes deterministically (base contract §3 dir 2). Used by `row_activation` (invoked with the activated row's `row_key`) and by inline cell edit commits on `editable` columns (invoked with the row id, column key, and new value). Row/cell WRITE-BACK to the slot happens through THIS tool's `writes_state` declaration (authored on the tool ref, not on the widget — base contract §4/§5); the table envelope has no `writes_state` of its own, so a skill cannot grant itself write access to a slot it does not own.

emits.description: Events this table posts to the agent bus (base contract §3, $defs/eventContract). Constrained to the table event set ($defs/tableEventName); each has a per-type `to_chat` default (all default `false` — table interactions are UI-local and should not clutter the transcript), overridable per event via the {name,to_chat} object form. Certification rejects duplicate event names. Omitted in the manifest → the type default set above.

columnDef.description: One typed column. `key` binds it to a field of the row record; `type` selects the cell renderer and its default alignment/format. All styling is via design-token variants only (align/tone) — no raw color/size (base contract §7).

key.description: The row-record field this column reads (snake_case). Must exist on the bound row records — a cross-artifact certification check against the slot's `record<…>` schema.

header.description: Localizable column heading (the `columnheader` accessible name). Omitted → a humanized form of `key`.

type.description: The cell TYPE for every cell in this column (base contract §2 'cells typed per column'). Drives formatting, default alignment, sort comparator, and the a11y presentation (e.g. `status`/`bool` always pair color with text+icon).

align.description: Cell/column text alignment, drawn from the closed style-variant vocabulary (start/center/end) — a design-token reference, never a raw value. Omitted → the type default: `end` for number/integer/currency/percent/duration, `center` for bool, `start` otherwise.

width.description: SEMANTIC column sizing — no raw px/rem is expressible (base contract §7). `auto` = size to content; `flex` = grow to absorb free space; `min` = clamp to the narrowest legible width and truncate. Concrete widths resolve first-party to spacing tokens.

sortable.description: Whether this column participates in sort. Omitted → the type default (sortable for text/number/integer/date/datetime/time/duration/currency/percent/enum; not for bool/status unless set). Ignored when `props.sortable:false`.

editable.description: When true, cells in this column offer inline edit (typed to `type`). Edits commit through `binds_tool` and are ENABLED only when the owning skill is the slot's writer-of-record; otherwise the affordance is present but disabled with the 'Managed by {writer}' description (base contract §5). A committed edit emits `cell_edited`.

truncate.description: Ellipsize overflowing cell content, exposing the full value via accessible tooltip/title. Omitted → true for text/enum/status, false for numeric/date/bool types.

format.description: Optional DATA-formatting hints (locale-aware; not styling). Which fields apply depends on `type` (e.g. `precision` for number/percent/currency, `date_style` for date/datetime) — the applicability is a certification check, not enforced structurally here.

values.description: The closed set of allowed cell values for `type:enum`/`type:status` columns. Used to validate/render chips and to key `labels`/`tone_map`. Optional for open enum display.

labels.description: Optional map from raw enum/status value → localizable DISPLAY text. Absent entries fall back to the raw value.

tone_map.description: For `type:status` only: map from status value → semantic `tone` variant (default/neutral/accent/positive/caution/danger), a design-token reference. The tone is NEVER the sole signal — the status cell always renders the value's label text and a per-tone icon alongside the color (WCAG 1.4.1 / base contract §8.2).

columnCellType.description: The closed set of cell types a column may declare. `bool` renders a check/dash glyph paired with a text label (never color-only). `status` renders a token-toned pill with label + icon (never hue-only). `duration` renders a human span (e.g. 12:30 / '12 min'). Date/time types render locale-aware. No `link`/`image`/`html` cell types exist — skills ship no remote content or markup (SPEC 9.1).

columnFormat.description: Closed set of locale-aware data-formatting hints. This is data presentation (number of decimals, date style), NOT styling — all visual styling remains token-driven via `style`/`align` (base contract §7).

grouping.description: Apply locale thousands grouping to numeric types.

currency_code.description: ISO-4217 code for `type:currency` (e.g. USD, EUR). Rendered with the locale's currency formatting.

unit_label.description: Localizable unit suffix/label for numeric cells (e.g. 'g', 'kcal').

true_label.description: Localizable text paired with the check glyph for a true `bool` cell (e.g. 'Yes'). The icon+text pairing keeps meaning non-hue-only.

false_label.description: Localizable text paired with the dash/× glyph for a false `bool` cell (e.g. 'No').

null_text.description: Text shown for a missing/empty cell value. Defaults first-party to an em-dash ('—'). Distinct from the table-level empty state (which fires only when there are no rows at all).

column.description: A column `key` to sort by. Must reference an existing, sortable column (certification check).

sortSpec.description: A single sort key. Comparison is by the column's `type` (numeric, lexical, temporal, or `values`-index order for enum/status), stable on `row_key`.

tableEventName.description: The closed table event vocabulary. `row_activated` — a row was activated (requires `row_activation`); `sort_changed` — the user changed sort order; `selection_changed` — the selected-row set changed (requires `selectable≠none`); `cell_edited` — an inline-editable cell was committed (requires an `editable` column + writer-of-record).

tableEventDecl.description: An emitted table event in shorthand (bare name) or canonical ({name,to_chat}) form — a name-restricted specialization of the base $defs/eventDecl. All table events default `to_chat:false` (UI-local); set `to_chat:true` to also post a system transcript line. Posting an event never auto-runs inference (base contract §3, SPEC 9.3 #4).

to_chat.description: Override the per-type default (false). true appends a system line to the transcript when the event fires.

bindsStateAcceptsKind.description: NON-NORMATIVE (documentation node). `table.binds_state` accepts a `list` slot whose element is a `record` — i.e. `list<record<…>>` (base contract §9, SPEC 8.3.4): the list supplies ROWS, each record supplies the fields the `columns[].key`s read. Vocabulary: base $defs/acceptsSlotKind value `list`. Because the concrete slot's kind lives in `shared_state`, this widget↔slot fit is a cross-artifact certification check (P1-04.5), not an in-envelope constraint. Slot↔slot compatibility of two skills' row records follows the record rule in base $defs/slotCompatibilityRule (same REQUIRED field set; extra optional fields widen away).

eventContract.description: NON-NORMATIVE (documentation node). EMITS: `row_activated`, `sort_changed`, `selection_changed`, `cell_edited` (see $defs/tableEventName), all `to_chat:false` by default; type-default emit set is [row_activated, sort_changed]. CONSUMES: the table subscribes to SLOT-UPDATE signals for its `binds_state` and every `reads_state` slot and re-renders on each (base contract §3 dir 3, routing keyed on slot NAME) — it does NOT subscribe to arbitrary named bus events, and it never receives UI code/directives from the model (SPEC 9.3 'Model↔UI bridge'). A tool result targeting this widget's `id` via `updates_widget` (base contract §4) also re-renders it.

lifecycleStates.description: NON-NORMATIVE (documentation node). Type-default copy for the mandatory states (base contract §6; overridable via the envelope `states` field, never suppressible). loading → label 'Loading table…' (determinate progress when a row count is known, else indeterminate — never a blank grid). empty → label 'No rows yet.' (bound OK, zero rows; distinct from a per-cell null_text). error → label 'Couldn't load the table.', hint 'Try again.' with a retry affordance; structured tool-execution errors are surfaced, never swallowed (SPEC 8.4). read-only → renders live with all edit/inline affordances disabled and a 'Managed by {writer}' description (base contract §5). placeholder → for an unknown/too-new library version, the first-party placeholder panel (base contract §11), terminal.

a11yContract.description: NON-NORMATIVE (documentation node). The MECHANICAL a11y contract the first-party `table` implementation guarantees and the X-A11Y gate enforces in BOTH themes on every state (base contract §8) — not waivable by a skill; skills may override only accessible name/description via `a11y`. ROLES: a static table (sortable:false, selectable:none, no editable/actionable rows) uses native `table`/`row`/`columnheader`/`rowheader`/`cell` semantics with a `<caption>` from props.caption; an INTERACTIVE table (sortable, selectable, editable, or row_activation) uses `grid` semantics with `gridcell`/`columnheader` and `aria-rowcount`/`aria-colcount` (full counts under pagination/virtualization) plus `aria-rowindex`/`aria-colindex`. KEYBOARD: Tab moves focus INTO the grid to a single roving-tabindex cell; Arrow keys move cell-to-cell; Home/End to row ends, Ctrl+Home/End to grid corners; PageUp/PageDown page when paginated. Enter/Space on a `columnheader` toggles/cycles its sort (asc→desc→none) and updates `aria-sort` (ascending/descending/none) which is announced; on a row it activates it (row_activation) or toggles selection (selectable). Editable cells enter edit mode on Enter/F2 and commit on Enter / cancel on Escape. FOCUS ORDER: header row before body; body rows in current VISUAL (sorted) order; a single tab stop for the whole grid (roving tabindex), visible focus ring from motion/focus tokens. LIVE REGION: out-of-band slot updates (a tool writing new/changed rows) announce via a polite live region ('Table updated, N rows') — the type-default `a11y.live_region` is `polite`; time-critical semantics are not a table concern. NO HUE-ONLY MEANING (WCAG 1.4.1): sort direction shows an arrow glyph + `aria-sort` (not color); `status` cells pair `tone_map` color with the value's label and a per-tone icon; `bool` cells pair color with a check/dash glyph and true/false label. CONTRAST: header/body/zebra/selected/focus/status token pairs are AA-verified PER THEME (a token passing on dark may fail on light — the --sulphur-ink precedent, P1-01.4). Text scales/zooms without loss (X-A11Y.3); transitions collapse under OS reduce-motion (base contract §8.6).

runtimeState.description: NON-NORMATIVE (documentation node — NOT authored in the manifest; produced by the core/renderer, base contract §2). The typed runtime projection the `table` renders: the resolved rows and interaction state. Session-scoped by default (SPEC 9.9).

rows.description: The current row records — the live value of the `binds_state` `list<record<…>>` slot, in display (sorted) order.

sort.description: Active sort keys (one entry unless multi_sort).

selection.description: Selected rows by `row_key` value (empty unless selectable≠none).

readonly.description: True when the owning skill is not the slot's writer-of-record; edit affordances are disabled (base contract §5).

writer.description: The writer-of-record skill id/name, surfaced in the 'Managed by {writer}' description when readonly.

error.description: Structured tool-execution error surfaced in the error state (SPEC 8.4).

props.caption: Calories and macros for each ingredient in the recipe.
